name: Steno
description: 洛杉矶诉讼AI和法庭记录初创公司，提供Transcript Genius产品，利用生成式AI分析案件笔录、索引证词并加速律师策略构建。
description_en: Los Angeles-based litigation AI and court reporting startup offering Transcript Genius, which uses generative AI to analyze case transcripts, index testimony, and accelerate attorney strategy building.
why_matters: 2026年3月25日获Savano Capital Partners领投$49M C轮，总融资达$150M，每月服务数千家律所，与纯软件法律AI不同，Steno兼具法庭记录服务和科技，提供真实诉讼数据优势，推动AmLaw 200市场渗透。
why_matters_en: Raised $49M Series C on March 25, 2026 led by Savano Capital Partners, bringing total to $150M; serves thousands of law firms monthly, differentiates from pure-software legal AI by combining court reporting services with tech for real litigation data access.
latest_news: 2026-03-25: Savano Capital Partners领投$49M C轮
latest_news_en: 2026-03-25: $49M Series C led by Savano Capital Partners

why_matters: YC W26被业界观察人士预测将产生20家独角兽企业（~10%命中率，远高于YC历史平均4.5%）。Rebel Fund的机器学习算法显示35%的W26初创公司评分在YC历史前20%，创历史新高。14家公司在Demo Day前达到$1M ARR是YC历史最高纪录，其中一家已达$27M ARR
why_matters_en: Industry observers predict W26 will produce 20 unicorns (~10% hit rate vs YC historical 4.5%). Rebel Fund's ML algorithm shows 35% of W26 startups score in top 20% of all YC companies ever—unprecedented. Record 14 companies hit $1M ARR by Demo Day, with one already at $27M ARR

top_comments_sample: Very cool idea closing the loop between simulation and real hardware. Have you found the MCP-driven workflow reliable enough for repeated testing cycles, or does it still need manual verification at key steps?
Nice! Doing something similar with a Jumperless so that the model can reconfigure the circuit on the fly.

Beware. I had Claude code with opus building boards and using spice simulations. It completely hallucinated the capabilities of the board and made some pretty crazy claims like I had just stumbled onto the secret hardware billion dollar project that every home needed. None of the boards worked and I had to just do the project in codex. Opus seemed too busy congratulating itself to realize it produced gibberish.
I've found that having LLMs work with mermaid diagrams makes describing and modifying circuits less annoying.

Hit this exact wall six months back building Claude Code stuff for KiCad review[1]. First pass let Claude read .kicad_sch directly via grep/read. It happily invented pin numbers that didn't exist. Rewrote it with Python analyzers that spit out JSON, now Claude just reads the JSON, problem mostly went away. Curious how spicelib-mcp handles models that aren't in the bundled library. Do you pass the .lib path as a tool arg, or does the server own a registry? [1] https://github.com/aklofas/kicad-happy
Measure with a micrometer, mark with a pencil, cut with an axe.

Really nice. My mother is an applied Physics teacher, and she told me they had a hard time at work figuring out how they could connect their teaching material to LLM in a relevant way. This should be useful to her.
Heh, this is like the last thing you need claude for. I mean, you have eyes and brain.
> SPICE (Simulation Program with Integrated Circuit Emphasis) is a general-purpose, open-source analog electronic circuit simulator. [1] 1: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SPICE
this kind of thing is super cool to close the loop. waiting for FPAA to get better so we can vibecode analog circuits https://www.eetimes.com/podcasts/making-analog-chip-designs-...

community_verdict.summary: 评论者赞赏将模拟与真实硬件结合的想法，认为这能关闭回路并对教育如物理教学有实际用途。一些用户分享类似项目经验，并讨论LLM在电路设计中的挑战，如幻觉和需要额外验证或工具如JSON解析器。整体反馈积极但指出可靠性问题和手动干预的必要性。 用户反馈既包含认可，也提出了执行层面的质疑。 对 PM 来说，关键是验证需求强度与可持续增长。
